phaseMemory.phase1GaveUs: Phase 1 gave us the project control tower, saved views, derived PM signals, weekly reviews, and durable roadmap memory.
phaseMemory.phase2Added: Phase 2 gave us structured execution data: decisions, work packets, tasks, blockers, throughput, and weekly execution history.
phaseMemory.phase3WillUse: Phase 3 will use those combined project + execution + research + skill + tool signals to recommend what to resume, finish, defer, or investigate next.
phaseMemory.phase3Brief: Phase 3 will use those combined project + execution + research + skill + tool signals to recommend what to resume, finish, defer, or investigate next.

phaseMemory.phase1GaveUs: Phase 1 gave us the project control tower, saved views, derived PM signals, weekly reviews, and durable roadmap memory.
phaseMemory.phase2Added: Phase 2 gave us structured execution data: decisions, work packets, tasks, blockers, throughput, and weekly execution history.
phaseMemory.phase3Added: Phase 3 gave us structured recommendation memory, recommendation-run history, and reviewed cross-database link intelligence.
phaseMemory.phase4Brief: Phase 4 will evaluate premium-native Notion overlays such as dashboards, reminder automations, synced databases, and custom agents only after the phase-3 recommendation engine is stable, trusted, and worth augmenting.
phaseMemory.phase5Brief: Phase 5 will bring in external signals such as repo, deploy, calendar, or workflow telemetry so recommendations can incorporate real execution evidence beyond Notion records.

portfolio.name: Portfolio Dashboard
portfolio.notes: Dashboard view shell exists, but the live workspace still shows an upgrade prompt before meaningful dashboard composition.

execution.name: Execution Dashboard
execution.notes: Dashboard view shell exists, but the live workspace still shows an upgrade prompt before meaningful dashboard composition.

projectReviewReminder.name: Project Review Reminder
projectReviewReminder.notes: Desired state is recorded in repo config; live rollout is tracked separately from canonical PM state.
projectReviewReminder.deferReason: Native reminder setup is UI-only and intentionally deferred until the premium workspace posture is stable.

decisionRevisitReminder.name: Decision Revisit Reminder
decisionRevisitReminder.notes: Decision-owner nudges stay outside canonical state transitions.
decisionRevisitReminder.deferReason: Native reminder setup is UI-only and intentionally deferred until the premium workspace posture is stable.

weeklyRunReviewReminder.name: Weekly Run Review Reminder
weeklyRunReviewReminder.notes: Draft-review nudges should never publish or mutate recommendation state automatically.
weeklyRunReviewReminder.deferReason: Native reminder setup is UI-only and intentionally deferred until the premium workspace posture is stable.

githubDeliverySignals.name: GitHub Delivery Signals
githubDeliverySignals.notes: Keep any synced delivery signal pilot read-only and scoped to one project.
githubDeliverySignals.deferReason: Synced databases are not visibly available in the current workspace, so the read-only GitHub pilot remains documented but deferred.

weeklyNativeSummaryDraft.name: Weekly Native Summary Draft
weeklyNativeSummaryDraft.notes: Pilot remains human-reviewed and separate from canonical project or recommendation state.
weeklyNativeSummaryDraft.deferReason: Custom agents are visible, but the live summary-draft pilot is deferred until the dashboard layer proves worth augmenting.

phaseMemory.phase1GaveUs: Phase 1 gave us the project control tower, saved views, derived PM signals, weekly reviews, and durable roadmap memory.
phaseMemory.phase2Added: Phase 2 gave us structured execution data: decisions, work packets, tasks, blockers, throughput, and weekly execution history.
phaseMemory.phase3Added: Phase 3 gave us structured recommendation memory, recommendation-run history, and reviewed cross-database link intelligence.
phaseMemory.phase4Added: Phase 4 gave us stable native dashboards, in-Notion review nudges, and bounded premium-native pilots layered on top of the repo-owned operating system.
phaseMemory.phase5Brief: Phase 5 will bring in external repo, deploy, calendar, and workflow signals as additive recommendation inputs so the operating system can compare Notion memory with real execution telemetry.
phaseMemory.phase6Brief: Phase 6 will add cross-system governance and approval gates so recommendations and external signals can safely influence actions outside Notion without losing human control.

phaseMemory.phase1GaveUs: Phase 1 gave us the project control tower, saved views, derived PM signals, weekly reviews, and durable roadmap memory.
phaseMemory.phase2Added: Phase 2 gave us structured execution data: decisions, work packets, tasks, blockers, throughput, and weekly execution history.
phaseMemory.phase3Added: Phase 3 gave us structured recommendation memory, recommendation-run history, and reviewed cross-database link intelligence.
phaseMemory.phase4Added: Phase 4 gave us stable native dashboards, in-Notion review nudges, and bounded premium-native pilots layered on top of the repo-owned operating system.
phaseMemory.phase5Added: Phase 5 gave us structured external telemetry, project-to-source mappings, sync-run history, and recommendation enrichment from repo and deployment evidence.
phaseMemory.phase6Brief: Phase 6 will add webhook policy, identity boundaries, credential posture, replay and dedupe rules, approval gates, and audit requirements before any higher-trust integration or external mutation.
phaseMemory.phase7Brief: Phase 7 will allow tightly approved cross-system actions such as creating work items, annotating deploys, or writing back to external systems from trusted recommendations.

phaseMemory.phase1GaveUs: Phase 1 gave us the project control tower, saved views, derived PM signals, weekly reviews, and durable roadmap memory.
phaseMemory.phase2Added: Phase 2 gave us structured execution data: decisions, work packets, tasks, blockers, throughput, and weekly execution history.
phaseMemory.phase3Added: Phase 3 gave us structured recommendation memory, recommendation-run history, and reviewed cross-database link intelligence.
phaseMemory.phase4Added: Phase 4 gave us stable native dashboards, in-Notion review nudges, and bounded premium-native pilots layered on top of the repo-owned operating system.
phaseMemory.phase5Added: Phase 5 gave us structured external telemetry, project-to-source mappings, sync-run history, and recommendation enrichment from repo and deployment evidence.
phaseMemory.phase6Added: Phase 6 gave us cross-system governance: policy-backed approvals, shadow-mode webhook verification, receipt and delivery audit trails, and provider-safe trust boundaries.
phaseMemory.phase7Brief: Phase 7 will allow tightly approved GitHub issue/comment actions first, then expand only after the same approval and audit path is proven for additional providers.

phaseMemory.phase1GaveUs: Phase 1 gave us the project control tower, saved views, derived PM signals, weekly reviews, and durable roadmap memory.
phaseMemory.phase2Added: Phase 2 gave us structured execution data: decisions, work packets, tasks, blockers, throughput, and weekly execution history.
phaseMemory.phase3Added: Phase 3 gave us structured recommendation memory, recommendation-run history, and reviewed cross-database link intelligence.
phaseMemory.phase4Added: Phase 4 gave us stable native dashboards, in-Notion review nudges, and bounded premium-native pilots layered on top of the repo-owned operating system.
phaseMemory.phase5Added: Phase 5 gave us structured external telemetry, project-to-source mappings, sync-run history, and recommendation enrichment from repo and deployment evidence.
phaseMemory.phase6Added: Phase 6 gave us cross-system governance: policy-backed approvals, shadow-mode webhook verification, receipt and delivery audit trails, and provider-safe trust boundaries.
phaseMemory.phase7Added: Phase 7 gave us controlled actuation: approved GitHub issue/comment execution, dry-run-backed execution logs, deterministic idempotency, and compensation-aware external write handling.
phaseMemory.phase8Brief: Phase 8 will deepen the proven GitHub-first actuation lane into a mature issue and PR-comment workflow with stronger security, better operator experience, and richer GitHub feedback loops.
